'''''Shadows of the Empire'' 2''' was the second issue in the six-part [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (comic series)|''Shadows of the Empire'']] series of comics, and was released on [[June 1]], [[1996]] by [[Dark Horse Comics]].

{{Comics-stub}}

==Publisher's summary==
''Prince Xizor controls the largest merchant fleet in the galaxy—that makes him a valuable asset to the Empire and to the Emperor. And that makes him Darth Vader's hated enemy. As Luke, Leia, and Chewbacca head to Tatooine to rescue Han Solo, they get caught in the crossfire of the galaxy's deadliest rivalry… in the ''Shadows of the Empire''''.

==Plot summary==
{{Plot|comic}}

==Development==
The issue behins with Darth Vader testing his [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]] skills with a [[ASP-series labor droid/Legends|ASP-series droid]]. [[Ryder Windham]] suggested to [[John Wagner]] to begin the issue with Vader battling furiously a hopeless doomed combat droid, as an opportunity to see Vader in [[lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] action. It would also suggest that he is testing himself after the arm injury he suffered on [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]].<ref name="secrets">''[[The Secrets of Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire]]''</ref>

At first, Vader's dialogue in the scene would be with Talan, a fearsome "space nomadic savage" and hunter whom Vader kept captive with his family. Vader forces him to protect Luke Skywalker, threatening with the lives of his family. [[Lucasfilm]] replaced Talan with [[Wrenga Jixton]], based on a background character in {{Film|6}}, in order to tie more characters of the [[original trilogy]] into the ''Shadows'' saga.<ref name="secrets" />

The script in the Gall cantina scene wanted a sniper aiming at Boba Fett from a balcony; falling dust on Fett's helmet would alert him of the danger. Thumbnail art showed that the dust would seem unclear on the drawing and the narrative; the finished version shows clearly the sniper instead.<ref name="secrets" />

The final shape of several objects and vehicles, including the ''Outrider'', was determined while the issue was under development, forcing artist Kilian Plunkett to erase his drawings and remake them with the revised design.<ref name="gather">''[[The Secrets of Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire]]'', Part 5: Shadow Play, I. GATHERING OF THE TRIBES</ref>

Pencil art was completed shortly after the July [[1995]] [[Wikipedia:San Diego Comic-Con|San Diego Comic-Con]].<ref name="gather" />
